item.description: Colombia made a winning return to the FIFA World Cup by defeating debutants Uzbekistan 3-1.After failing to qualify for the 2022 c

item.title: Vodafone Fiji and FNRL mark 20-year partnership with launch of 2026 Vodafone Cup
item.description: Vodafone Fiji and the Fiji National Rugby League celebrated their 20 years of partnership today, as the launch of the 2026 Vodafone Cup competition was held at the HFC Bank Stadium. FNRL Chairman Rajen Singh says what began as a commitment to support rugby league has grown into one of the longest-standing sporting partnerships in Fiji, and this is one of the most enduring relationships anywhere in
item.content:encoded:
Vodafone Fiji and the Fiji National Rugby League celebrated their 20 years of partnership today, as the launch of the 2026 Vodafone Cup competition was held at the HFC Bank Stadium.

FNRL Chairman Rajen Singh says what began as a commitment to support rugby league has grown into one of the longest-standing sporting partnerships in Fiji, and this is one of the most enduring relationships anywhere in the Pacific.

Singh says he wants to acknowledge Vodafone Fiji for not only supporting rugby league, but investing in people's lives and giving young people hope, creating opportunities and providing pathways over two decades.

The Chairman says 44 teams will be participating in this year's Vodafone Cup with 11 women's team.

The competition kicks off this Saturday.

item.title: Vakatawa to start for Babaas against Springboks, Samusamuvodre named on bench
item.description: Swire Shipping Fijian Drua centre Virimi Vakatawa will start for the Barbarians in the midfield while Tuidraki Samusamuvodre is on the bench against the Springboks this Sunnday. Former All Blacks halfback TJ Perenara will captain the side with former Chiefs player Lachlan Boshier at vice captain.
item.content:encoded:
Swire Shipping Fijian Drua centre Virimi Vakatawa will start for the Barbarians in the midfield while Tuidraki Samusamuvodre is on the bench against the Springboks this Sunnday.

Former All Blacks halfback TJ Perenara will captain the side with former Chiefs player Lachlan Boshier at vice captain.

Scott Robertson has picked Argentine loose-head prop Mayco Vivas, Welsh hooker Elliot Dee, and Scottish tighthead D'Arcy Rae for his front row.

Englishman Alex Moon pairs up with Italian Franco Molina in the locks.

The loose forwards are Argentine Guido Petti and Boshier with Miracle Faiʻilagi at No. 8.

Perenara is at halfback with Tomas Albornoz at flyhalf.

The back three is made up of Scottish Duhan van der Merwe, Waratahs Andrew Kellaway and Stormers Warrick Gelant at fullback.

Vakatawa and Alex Nankivell are in the midfield.

On the bench are Leonel Oviedo, Oli Kebble, Pedro Delgado, Izack Rodda, Liam McConell, Santiago Arata, Harry Plummer and Samusamuvodre.

The Barbarians will face South Africa at 3am Sunday at Nelson Mandela Bay Stadium.

item.title: Ghana secure dramatic 1-0 victory over Panama
item.description: Ghana have secured a dramatic 95th minute 1-0 victory over Panama in their opening FIFA World Cup Group L match. After a scoreless first half, Caleb Yirenkyi scored the winner in the fifth minute of injury time. Caleb Yirenkyi plays in Denmark for Nordsjaelland.
item.content:encoded:
Ghana have secured a dramatic 95th minute 1-0 victory over Panama in their opening FIFA World Cup Group L match.

After a scoreless first half, Caleb Yirenkyi scored the winner in the fifth minute of injury time. Caleb Yirenkyi plays in Denmark for Nordsjaelland.

The win gives the Black Stars an early boost in their bid to reach the knockout stages.

Ghana are making their fifth FIFA World Cup appearance, having previously featured in 2006, 2010, 2014 and 2022. Their best performance came in 2010 when they reached the quarter-finals.

Panama are appearing in only their second FIFA World Cup after making their tournament debut in 2018 and are still chasing their first World Cup win.

Ghana will next face England at 8am next Wednesday before taking on Croatia at 9am next Sunday.

Meanwhile, Panama will meet Croatia at 11am next Wednesday before facing England at 9am next Sunday.

Earlier today, Portugal and DR Congo settled for a 1-all draw while England defeated Croatia 4-2.

Uzbekistan will take on Colombia at 2pm.

item.title: Harry Kane scores twice as England defeats Croatia 4-2
item.description: England has made a winning start to their FIFA World Cup campaign after defeating Croatia 4-2 in an entertaining Group L encounter, with captain Harry Kane scoring twice. Kane put England ahead from the penalty spot in the 12th minute, but Croatia drew level through Martin Baturina in the 36th minute.
item.content:encoded:
England has made a winning start to their FIFA World Cup campaign after defeating Croatia 4-2 in an entertaining Group L encounter, with captain Harry Kane scoring twice.

Kane put England ahead from the penalty spot in the 12th minute, but Croatia drew level through Martin Baturina in the 36th minute.

The England skipper struck again in the 42nd minute to restore his side's lead before Petar Musa equalised for Croatia in first-half injury time to send the teams into the break locked at 2-2.

England regained the advantage shortly after halftime when Jude Bellingham found the back of the net in the 47th minute, while Marcus Rashford added a fourth goal in the 85th minute to secure all three points.

The result gives England an early boost in Group L as they chase a place in the knockout stages.

England are featuring in their 17th FIFA World Cup and are aiming to win the tournament for the second time after lifting the trophy in 1966.

Croatia, who are making their seventh World Cup appearance, entered the tournament after impressive recent campaigns that saw them finish runners-up in 2018 and third in 2022.

England's next Group L match will be against Ghana next Wednesday at 8am before they face Panama next Sunday at 9am.

Croatia will take on Panama next Wednesday at 11am before meeting Ghana next Sunday at 9am in their remaining group fixtures.

Meanwhile, Portugal and DR Congo settled for a 1-all draw earlier this morning.

Ghana will face Panama at 11am while Uzbekistan will take on Colombia at 2pm.

item.description: DR Congo have fought back to level their FIFA World Cup Group K match against Portugal 1-1 just before halftime.Portugal took an e
